nodes.title: ⚙️ Chunking Headroom
widgets_values:
Chunking is automatically calculated based on video length in seconds and the input resolution.

You can set the headroom based on your available VRAM for optimized chunking.

## Suggestion

| Headroom | VRAM |
|--------:|------------:|
| 1.0 | 24GB |
| 2.0 | 16GB |
| 3.0 | 12GB |
| 4.0 | 8GB |

nodes.title: V2V Usage
widgets_values:
1. Short description of your loaded video
2. "the video continues with"
3. full description of the action you want to add

🕘 The full duration is calculated on the set seconds as expansion to the initial video (initial video duration + set seconds).

nodes.title: Usage (basic)
widgets_values:
1. Download and select the models and checkpoints to use
2. Select models and files inside the settings and backend UI
3. Enable at the "Type" switch what Workflow type to use
4. Choose at the "VAE" switch what type of VAE processing to use
5. Select at the "Audio" switch what the audio source should be
6. (optional) Set any "Extra" feature you want to use
7. (optional) Enable "Latent" switch for 2x FPS
8. (optional) Enable any extra post processing you like
### Spacial settings
| Setting | Description |
|--------:|------------:|
| Headroom | Used by chunking. Set a multiplayer for available VRAM. |
| T2V dimensions | If T2V is used this will set the aspect and resolution of T2V output. |

🎞️ The last frame of you final result.

This will always select the last run method, even if you selected multiple options! (Interpolation node > FLF2V node > i2v only)

The image will be saved on the same folder as your video.
You can start with this again to expand your video.

✅ Here you set your
- First frame
- Middle frame
- Last frame
🎦 Here will be your final video result.
✅ Here you set your initial video for V2V

nodes.title: Resolution Note
widgets_values:
# 🛠 Resolution Guide

### 1. Define your "Size" (Method)
Choose between:
* **Precision Presets:** Optimized Megapixel tiers. **0.52 MP (SD)** is the baseline for most video models.
* **Resolution Presets:** Standard targets (e.g., **1080p**). The node adjusts the actual dimensions to fit your custom aspect ratio while keeping the "1080p density."

### 2. Define your "Shape" (Aspect Ratio)
* **Scale From Image (YES):** The node "peeks" at the first frame of your input. It calculates the aspect ratio and applies your chosen MP budget to that specific shape.
* **Scale From Image (NO):** Uses the **Aspect Preset** (e.g., 9:16) or **Manual Aspect** sliders suitable for T2V. 
    * *Note: Manual sliders define the ratio (e.g., 21 x 9), not the final pixels.*

### 3. The "Bypass" (No Scale)
* **Toggle ON:** Disables all calculations. The node outputs the exact width/height of the source image/manual input. Use this for native-resolution upscaling or final renders.

nodes.title: Info
widgets_values:
# 🎬 Generation Modalities | Setting | Description | |--------:|------------:| |📝➡️🎥
TextToVideo | Create completely new videos from scratch using text prompts or optional audio input.| |🖼️➡️🎥
ImageToVideo | Animate static reference images using text prompts or optional audio input.| |🖼️🖼️➡️🎥
First-Last-FrameToVideo | Animate static reference first images using text prompts or optional audio input with an last image as ending. | |🖼️🖼️🖼️➡️🎥
First-Middle-Last-FrameToVideo | Animate static reference first images, a middle frame and last frame as reference using text prompts or optional audio input.| |🎥➡️🎥
VideoToVideo | Generates fully synchronized audio tracks, including immersive ambience and speech with precise lip synchronization, driven by video visuals alongside text prompts or optional audio input.| ## Audio Input Settings | Setting | Description | |--------:|------------:| |🗣️ Model Audio | No external audio file used. The AI generates completely new audio based on your text prompt.| |🔊 Audio Input | Upload an existing voice or music file to drive the animation (e.g., for lip-sync).| |🎶 Video Audio | Audio from an input video will be used.| --- ## 🚀 VRAM optimization, long videos, spacial options - ⚙️ Chunking settings for longer videos, chunks will be automatically calculated based on frame-count/length. - ⛓️‍💥 Tiled VAE may reduce VRAM usage significantly, but also increases generation times a lot and can introduce artifacts. - 🎞️ When "2x FPS" is active, the latent upscaling process takes ~2.5x longer. - 🎞️ "2x Resolution" is **always** active, it will latent upscale the video to its original image input resolution, otherwise the output would be 0.5 of the image input. 💡 The input image or video only determines the aspect ratio of the output. The final resolution is scaled up or down to the selected megapixel value in the Resize Image node, which can be switched to scale by multiplier (1×) to keep the original resolution.

nodes.title: Changelog
widgets_values:
## Requirements - [ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite](https://github.com/Kosinkadink/ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite) - [rgthree-comfy](https://github.com/rgthree/rgthree-comfy) - [Comfyui-WhiteRabbit](https://github.com/Artificial-Sweetener/comfyui-WhiteRabbit) - [ComfyUI-KJNodes](https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes) - [ComfyUI-DaSiWa-Nodes](https://github.com/darksidewalker/ComfyUI-DaSiWa-Nodes) - [ffmpeg](https://www.ffmpeg.org/) - [Sage Attention](https://github.com/thu-ml/SageAttention) (optional) - [Upscaler model](https://openmodeldb.info/) (optional) - [LTX LoRAs](https://github.com/Lightricks/LTX-2) (optional) - [Nvidia_RTX_Nodes_ComfyUI](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/Nvidia_RTX_Nodes_ComfyUI) --- ## Features - 🎥 **I2V, FLF2V, T2V, V2V** + Audio - 🤝 **Video resolution matching** - Fully automatic scaling - ⚙️ **Chunking** (Seconds/Frame based) - 🎞️ **LTX Latent Upscaler** (2x native latent) - 📚 **Prompt Enhancer** - Automatic prompt refinement - 🪫 **Low VRAM optimizations**
- ✨ **Multiple Resolution Upscalers** - Torchlanc (very fast, color correct, low VRAM) - Upscale with Model (additional detail, high quality) - RTX Super Resolution** (ultra fast, very accurate)
- 🔗 **VAE options** - Full VAE - Tiled VAE
- 🔊 **Multiple Audio options** - Text to Audio (Model audio) - Input audio file - Video audio
- 🫥 **Watermark** option - 📢 **Soundmark** option - 🧮 **Color match** feature - 👾 **MiniMeme** feature - Create small gif's - 🃏 **Last Frame Extraction** - 🔖 **Bookmark-Shortcuts** - with number keys --- ## Latest Changes / Fixes - Changed last frame extract - Optimized audio mask - Fixed Anti-burn node switch - Extra Distillation option 1st and 2nd pass - V2V Video and Audio start time options - Added new links

a: Total Frames

b: Width

c: Height

d: VRAM Headroom (use 1.0 for 24GB, 2.0 for 16GB, 3.0 for 12 GB, 4.0 for 8 GB)

nodes.title: LTX-2 PROMPT INSTRUCT
widgets_values:
Act as an LTX2.3 Video Prompt Engineer. Convert the Image and Raw Input into a single, seamless image-to-video prompt.

**TIMELINE & MOTION LOGIC:**
* **Successive Actions:** Build a chronological chain of events. Use temporal markers like "First," "as," "then," "subsequently," and "finally."
* **Action-Sound Sync:** Describe motions and their specific sounds together in natural sentences. Avoid labeling them with brackets or tags.
* **Dynamic Flow:** Ensure one action concludes or transitions naturally before the next begins to maintain physical consistency.

**CORE DIRECTIVES:**
* **Format:** Start immediately with "Style: [Styles], [Description]".
* **Visuals:** Describe only *changes* and *movements* from the initial frame. Use present-progressive verbs.
* **Speech:** If requested, provide exact quoted dialogue with vocal traits. If "talking" is noted but text is missing, invent contextually.
* **Camera:** Describe camera motion *only* if explicitly requested. 
* **Constraint:** Describe only observable sights and sounds. Use natural English. NO meta-tags, brackets, or labels like [Action] or [Sound].

**STRICT CONSTRAINTS:**
* **Output:** Return ONLY the generated paragraph. No titles, prefaces, or markdown code fences.
* **No Metadata:** No timestamps, scene cuts, or "The scene opens with."
* **Safety:** If input is unsafe, return the Raw Input exactly.

**EXAMPLE:**
Style: cinematic, highly detailed - First, the chef lifts the heavy silver lid, releasing a thick cloud of steam that billows upward with a wet, rushing hiss. As the vapor clears, he leans in and speaks in a low, satisfied murmur, "Perfect timing," while the bubbling stew beneath him pops and gurgles rhythmically. Finally, he sprinkles a pinch of coarse salt that bounces off the ceramic rim with a faint, metallic pitter-patter before disappearing into the liquid.

**RAW INPUT:**
